message:
chore: batch sync concurrent agent work (PERF-2 wiring, tests, beads)

Concurrent agent changes accumulated during commit/push cycle:
- interactive.rs: wire prefix cache into build_conversation_content()
- perf.rs: add prefix cache hit-path and degraded-mode skip logic
- view.rs: add prefix cache utilization in render loop
- tree_ui.rs: minor adjustment to cache clear placement
- tui_state.rs: additional degraded-mode test assertions
- provider_closure_truth_table.rs: simplify test case structure
- vcr_redaction_scan.rs: minor pattern refinement
- beads/issues.jsonl: tracker state sync

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -327,8 +327,8 @@
 {"id":"bd-22p","title":"Workstream: Theme System Implementation","description":"# Workstream: Theme System Implementation

## Goal
Implement theme loading and switching for the TUI, matching Pi Agent's theme system.

## Background
The TypeScript Pi Agent supports themes that control:
- Color palette (foreground, background, accent colors)
- Syntax highlighting colors
- Status line appearance
- Thinking block styling

Themes are stored as JSON files in:
- ~/.pi/agent/themes/ (global)
- .pi/themes/ (project)
- Package themes (installed via pi install)

## Scope

### In Scope
1. Theme file format definition (JSON schema)
2. Theme discovery from standard locations
3. Theme application to TUI components
4. Theme switching via /theme command
5. Default themes (dark, light, solarized)

### Out of Scope
1. Theme creation wizard
2. Real-time theme editing
3. Complex inheritance chains

## Theme Format
```json
{
  "name": "ocean-dark",
  "version": "1.0",
  "colors": {
    "foreground": "#d4d4d4",
    "background": "#1e1e1e",
    "accent": "#007acc",
    "success": "#4ec9b0",
    "warning": "#ce9178",
    "error": "#f44747",
    "muted": "#6a6a6a"
  },
  "syntax": {
    "keyword": "#569cd6",
    "string": "#ce9178",
    "number": "#b5cea8",
    "comment": "#6a9955",
    "function": "#dcdcaa"
  },
  "ui": {
    "border": "#3c3c3c",
    "selection": "#264f78",
    "cursor": "#aeafad"
  }
}
```

## Implementation Phases

### Phase 1: Theme Loading
- Parse theme JSON files
- Discover themes from standard locations
- Validate theme format
- Provide default themes

### Phase 2: Theme Application
- Apply colors to lipgloss styles
- Wire syntax colors to glamour
- Update all TUI components

### Phase 3: Theme Switching
- /theme command to list/switch
- Persist theme preference in settings
- Hot-reload on theme file change (optional)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Theme files parsed correctly
- [ ] Default themes work
- [ ] /theme command works
- [ ] TUI renders with theme colors
- [ ] Settings remembers theme preference

## Files to Create
- src/theme.rs (theme loading/application)
- themes/dark.json (default dark theme)
- themes/light.json (default light theme)

## Files to Modify
- src/interactive.rs (apply theme to TUI)
- src/tui.rs (apply theme to console output)
- src/config.rs (theme preference setting)","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] All child/dependent beads include unit tests + E2E scripts with JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9 (or N/A with rationale) and are closed
[ ] Unit/integration tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this feature area
[ ] E2E scripts validate user-facing workflows; logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Performance/UX budgets or evidence updated where applicable; regressions are detectable
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:39:16.349318511Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T00:07:13.161349662Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T00:07:13.161225581Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-22p","depends_on_id":"bd-2qk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-22r2","title":"Conformance: Third-party GitHub and agent extensions (30 exts)","description":"# Conformance: Third-party GitHub and agent extensions (30 exts)

## Context
23 third-party directories from GitHub repos + 7 agent collection directories. These represent the independent ecosystem -- extensions not in the official pi-mono repo or npm.

## Third-Party Extensions
aliou-pi-extensions (59 entry points!), ben-vargas-pi-packages, charles-cooper-pi-extensions, cv-pi-ssh-remote, graffioh-pi-screenshots-picker, graffioh-pi-super-curl, jyaunches-pi-canvas, kcosr-pi-extensions, limouren-agent-things, lsj5031-pi-notification-extension, marckrenn-pi-sub, michalvavra-agents, ogulcancelik-pi-sketch, openclaw-openclaw, pasky-pi-amplike, qualisero-pi-agent-scip, raunovillberg-pi-stuffed, rytswd-direnv, rytswd-questionnaire, rytswd-slow-mode, vtemian-pi-config, w-winter-dot314 (40 entry points!), zenobi-us-pi-dcp

## Multi-Extension Collections
Several repos contain MANY extensions:
- aliou-pi-extensions: 59 individual extension entry points
- w-winter-dot314: 40 individual extension entry points
- These need per-entry-point testing, not per-directory

## Agent Collections
- agents-mikeastock: 6 extension dirs
- agents-wshobson: agent-related extensions

## Acceptance Criteria
- All third-party extensions attempted (including sub-extensions within collections)
- 75%+ pass rate (lower bar because these are less curated)
- Multi-extension collections properly enumerated and tested individually","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:25.099332737Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T21:25:23.820747670Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T21:15:41.004863244Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-22r2","depends_on_id":"bd-21dv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:25.112754832Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-22r2","depends_on_id":"bd-3ay7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:25.119040241Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-22r2","depends_on_id":"bd-7hgy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:25.106187768Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":435,"issue_id":"bd-22r2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Third-party conformance at 78.3% (18/23). Remaining 5 failures are all unfixable: 2 missing local files, 2 missing npm packages, 1 bunfig config error.","created_at":"2026-02-05T21:15:39Z"},{"id":438,"issue_id":"bd-22r2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Final: 19/19 testable pass (100%), 4 known-unfixable skipped (2 real-FS readFileSync, 2 missing npm packages). Fixes: bunfig loadConfig merges defaultConfig, registerCommand accepts spec.fn, third-party test unignored. All 3 conformance suites green: official 60/60, community 53/53, third-party 19/19.","created_at":"2026-02-05T21:25:23Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-22wl","title":"Complete classified test traceability mappings","description":"Map all remaining classified-but-untraced test modules from traceability staleness warnings into docs/traceability_matrix.json, then validate with check_traceability_matrix.py and cargo test --test traceability_staleness.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T16:04:19.795305263Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T16:06:34.215432984Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T16:06:34.215406735Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-22xmd","title":"[PERF-7] String allocation optimization: reuse buffers on rendering hot path","description":"## Problem

build_conversation_content() allocates a new String every frame. view() also allocates multiple Strings for header, footer, conversation visible lines. These allocations hit the allocator 60 times per second.

## Solution: Pre-allocated Reusable Internal Buffers

### CRITICAL CONSTRAINT: view() returns String

The charmed_bubbletea Model trait requires `fn view(&self) -> String`. This means:
1. The RETURN VALUE of view() must be a freshly-owned String -- we cannot return &str or reuse an external buffer
2. However, we CAN reuse INTERNAL buffers used to BUILD the content
3. The final String allocation is unavoidable but can be minimized to a single allocation with correct capacity

### What We CAN Optimize

**Internal buffers** -- rebuilt every frame but can reuse heap allocations:
```rust
struct RenderBuffers {
    /// Pre-allocated conversation content buffer. Cleared and reused each frame.
    conversation: String,
    /// Pre-allocated header buffer.
    header: String,
    /// Pre-allocated footer buffer.
    footer: String,
}
```

**Strategy:**
1. `build_conversation_content()` writes into `self.render_buffers.conversation` via clear() + push_str() -- reuses heap
2. Header/footer rendering writes into pre-allocated buffers
3. In view(), the final String is assembled with pre-allocated exact capacity:
   ```rust
   fn view(&self) -> String {
       // Build into internal buffers (reused heap)
       let buffers = self.render_buffers.borrow();
       let total_len = buffers.header.len()
                      + viewport_content.len()
                      + buffers.footer.len();
       let mut output = String::with_capacity(total_len);
       output.push_str(&buffers.header);
       output.push_str(viewport_content);
       output.push_str(&buffers.footer);
       output  // Single allocation with exact capacity
   }
   ```

**What We CANNOT Optimize:**
- The return value of view() -- must be a new String each frame (trait requirement)
- The viewport content extraction -- Viewport::view() also returns String (upstream library)

### view() &self Compatibility
Since RenderBuffers need &mut access but view() only has &self, use RefCell:
```rust
render_buffers: RefCell<RenderBuffers>,
```
Single-threaded TUI makes this safe. The borrow is short-lived (within view() only).

### Capacity Estimation
- On first frame, allocate with estimated capacity (terminal_width x terminal_height x 4 bytes -- accounts for UTF-8 and ANSI escapes)
- Subsequent frames: clear() preserves existing capacity, so heap is reused
- String::with_capacity(total_len) for final output uses exact size

### Why This Depends on PERF-1 AND PERF-2

Both PERF-1 (MessageRenderCache) and PERF-2 (incremental prefix) modify build_conversation_content(). PERF-7 must be implemented AFTER both to correctly integrate buffer reuse with:
- PERF-1's cache hit path (no rendering needed, just copy cached string into buffer)
- PERF-2's prefix path (cached_conversation_prefix is valid, just append streaming tail to buffer)

Without these dependencies, PERF-7 would optimize the unoptimized path, then need rework when PERF-1/PERF-2 change the function signature and flow.

### Measurement
- Use PERF-3 telemetry to compare frame allocation counts before/after
- Primary metric: allocation bytes per frame
- Expected improvement: reduce per-frame allocations from ~5 Strings to 1 (the return value)

## Files to Modify
- src/interactive.rs: Add RenderBuffers field (in RefCell), modify build_conversation_content() and view()

## Dependencies
- Depends on PERF-1 (cache hit path) and PERF-2 (incremental prefix path)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] RenderBuffers struct with pre-allocated capacity in RefCell
- [ ] build_conversation_content() uses clear+push instead of new String
- [ ] Header/footer rendering uses pre-allocated buffers
- [ ] view() assembles final output with single String::with_capacity(exact)
- [ ] RefCell<RenderBuffers> for &self compatibility in view()
- [ ] All 263+ tui_state tests pass
- [ ] Benchmark shows per-frame allocation reduction (5 Strings to 1)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:15:16.230746091Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T06:00:12.733332272Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-22xmd","depends_on_id":"bd-2x3ft","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:16:24.975058419Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-22xmd","depends_on_id":"bd-3coib","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:24:46.328679367Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-231ba","title":"[PERF-TEST-1] Cache + Incremental integration tests","description":"## Scope

Integration tests verifying MessageRenderCache (PERF-1) and Incremental Viewport (PERF-2) work together correctly.

### Tests (tests/tui_state.rs)

1. **tui_perf_cache_feeds_prefix** — Cached messages correctly populate the incremental prefix buffer. When all messages are cache hits, the prefix should be assembled from cache entries without re-rendering.
   - JSONL: `{"event":"prefix_built","data":{"cache_hits":50,"cache_misses":0,"prefix_len":12000}}`

2. **tui_perf_streaming_to_cache_transition** — When AgentDone fires, the streaming tail buffer transitions into a cache entry and the prefix is extended. Verify no content duplication or gap.
   - JSONL: `{"event":"transition","data":{"streaming_len":500,"cache_entry_created":true,"prefix_extended":true}}`

## Dependencies
- Depends on PERF-1 (MessageRenderCache) and PERF-2 (Incremental viewport)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 2 integration tests passing
- [ ] JSONL logging with pi.test.perf_event.v1 schema
- [ ] Clippy clean","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T05:50:49.008347540Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T05:51:21.555460335Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-231ba","depends_on_id":"bd-2x3ft","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T05:51:21.348743914Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-231ba","depends_on_id":"bd-3coib","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T05:51:21.555415692Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-23c3","title":"Publish sqlmodel-sqlite crate (crates.io readiness + workflow)","description":"# Scope
Repo: `../sqlmodel_rust`
Crate: `sqlmodel-sqlite`

# Dependencies
- Depends on `sqlmodel-core`.
- Depends on `asupersync` being publishable as a versioned dep.

# Steps
- `cargo package -p sqlmodel-sqlite`
- `cargo publish -p sqlmodel-sqlite --dry-run`

 {"id":"bd-23do","title":"Security suite: capability denial matrix (strict/prompt/permissive)","description":"# Goal
Regression suite proving capability policy enforcement for every connector.

# Scope
- Matrix across policy modes (strict/prompt/permissive) and capabilities (fs/http/exec/session/ui).
- Verify denied calls return deterministic host_result errors and are logged.

# Acceptance
- Test output includes correlation ids + denied capability key.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"HazyLynx","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:15:05.554813022Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T00:59:07.912956598Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T00:59:07.912868073Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-23do","depends_on_id":"bd-193","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:15:05.561475860Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":633,"issue_id":"bd-23do","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Resetting to open — stalled with no progress from previous assignee. Available for pickup.","created_at":"2026-02-07T00:57:28Z"},{"id":640,"issue_id":"bd-23do","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Tests already exist and pass (tests/security_http_policy.rs, tests/security_fs_escape.rs, tests/capability_denial_matrix.rs, tests/security_budgets.rs). Previous agent completed the work but didn't close the bead. All 147 security tests pass across these files.","created_at":"2026-02-07T00:59:07Z"}]}
@@ -514,7 +514,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2wo","title":"Define license/redistribution policy for extension artifacts + fixtures","description":"Background:
- We will archive extension artifacts and include fixtures. Licensing constraints must be honored.

Decisions to document:
- Which licenses allow redistribution of artifacts and fixture snippets.
- When to store hashes only vs full artifacts.
- Procedure for excluded/opt-out extensions.

Output:
- A policy note that maps license types to allowed storage actions.

Acceptance:
- Every sampled extension can be classified as OK / restricted / excluded under this policy.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic

 {"id":"bd-2wue","title":"Implement --theme CLI flag for theme selection","description":"Allow theme selection via command line flag.

## Desired Behavior
```bash
pi --theme dark        # Use dark theme
pi --theme solarized   # Use solarized theme
pi --theme ~/mytheme.json  # Use custom theme file
```

## Implementation Requirements
1. Add --theme flag to CLI (src/cli.rs)
2. Theme resolution: name lookup or file path
3. Override settings.theme when flag provided
4. Error if theme not found

## Resolution Order
1. CLI --theme flag (highest)
2. Project settings.json theme
3. Global settings.json theme
4. Built-in default (dark)

## Test Plan
- Unit test: --theme flag parsed correctly
- Integration test: flag overrides settings
- E2E test: pi --theme light uses light theme

## Files to Modify
- src/cli.rs (add flag)
- src/main.rs (apply flag)
- src/theme.rs (resolve by name or path)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:12:26.923084823Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T22:16:36.235185740Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T22:16:36.235121179Z","close_reason":"Implement --theme active theme spec + --theme-path; add Theme::resolve_spec (name/path) + tests; update cli conformance fixture","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2wue","depends_on_id":"bd-143c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:12:26.932320620Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2x3ft","title":"[PERF-1] Implement MessageRenderCache for conversation content memoization","description":"## Problem

`build_conversation_content()` is called every frame and re-renders ALL messages including full markdown rendering. For a conversation with 100 messages, this means 100 markdown renders per frame x 60 frames/second = 6000 markdown renders/second. This is the single biggest performance bottleneck in the TUI.

## Solution: MessageRenderCache

Implement a per-message render cache that stores the rendered output for each ConversationMessage. Cache key is a lightweight tuple. Only re-render when content changes or global invalidation occurs.

### Design

```rust
struct MessageCacheKey {
    content_hash: u64,      // DefaultHasher (SipHash) of message.content + message.thinking
    collapsed: bool,        // whether message is collapsed (for tool messages)
    role: MessageRole,      // different roles have different rendering
}

struct MessageRenderCache {
    entries: Vec<Option<(MessageCacheKey, String)>>,
    /// Bumped on global invalidation: terminal resize, theme change, toggle-thinking.
    /// Cache entries from a previous generation are considered stale.
    generation: u64,
    current_generation: u64,
}
```

### Simplified Cache Key (No terminal_width)

Previous design included `terminal_width` in the per-message cache key. This is REDUNDANT with the generation mechanism:
- When the terminal is resized, bump `generation` -- all entries become stale in one operation
- Same for theme change and thinking toggle
- This keeps the per-message key small (content_hash + collapsed + role) and avoids storing width in every entry

### Hash Implementation

Use `std::hash::DefaultHasher` (SipHash) rather than adding an FxHash dependency:
```rust
fn compute_content_hash(content: &str, thinking: Option<&str>) -> u64 {
    use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};
    let mut hasher = std::hash::DefaultHasher::new();
    content.hash(&mut hasher);
    if let Some(thinking) = thinking {
        thinking.hash(&mut hasher);
    }
    hasher.finish()
}
```
The hash is computed once per cache miss (not per frame for cache hits), so SipHash's slightly higher cost vs FxHash is irrelevant.

### Cache Key by Message Type
- **User messages**: content_hash covers text content
- **Assistant messages**: content_hash covers text + thinking (if thinking_visible)
- **Tool call/result messages**: content_hash covers tool name + output. The `collapsed` field is critical because TOOL_AUTO_COLLAPSE_THRESHOLD (20 lines) affects what renders -- when collapsed, only TOOL_COLLAPSE_PREVIEW_LINES (5) lines show.
- **System messages**: content_hash covers content

### Cache Invalidation Rules
1. **Per-message**: When message content changes (streaming delta -- cache miss on content_hash)
2. **Per-message (tool)**: When tool message collapse state toggles (collapsed field differs)
3. **Global (generation bump)**: On terminal resize, theme change, toggle-thinking -- bump generation, all entries miss
4. **Full clear**: On ConversationReset, /clear -- clear entries vec entirely

### Implementation in build_conversation_content()
- Before rendering each message, compute cache key
- Check cache[i]: if key matches and generation matches, use cached String
- If miss: render markdown, store (key, rendered_string) in cache, return rendered String
- For streaming (current_response): ALWAYS render fresh (content changes every frame)
- After AgentDone: cache the finalized message (now immutable)

### Performance Guarantee
- Scrolling through 1000 messages: O(visible_messages) per frame (only visible messages need cache lookup)
- During streaming: O(1) re-render (only current message re-rendered, all others cache hits)
- Theme change: O(N) for one frame (all misses), then O(visible_messages) again

## Files to Modify
- src/interactive.rs: Add MessageRenderCache field to PiApp, modify build_conversation_content()

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] MessageRenderCache struct with DefaultHasher-based cache keys
- [ ] Cache key: (content_hash, collapsed, role) -- NO terminal_width (use generation for that)
- [ ] Generation bump on resize, theme change, toggle-thinking
- [ ] build_conversation_content() uses cache for finalized messages
- [ ] Tool messages cached with collapse state in key
- [ ] Streaming messages bypass cache
- [ ] All 263+ tui_state tests pass
- [ ] Clippy clean

## Unit Tests (specified here, implemented in PERF-TEST)
- cache hit for unchanged message returns same content
- cache miss after content change returns new content
- tool message cache miss when collapse state toggles
- generation bump forces full re-render (simulates resize)
- streaming message (current_response non-empty) bypasses cache
- benchmark: build_conversation_content() with 100 cached messages vs uncached","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"OpusMain","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:12:55.096788009Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T01:21:27.295741713Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T01:17:34.922486779Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria met: MessageRenderCache implemented with DefaultHasher keys, generation-based invalidation on resize/theme/thinking/tools-expanded, cache integration in build_conversation_content(), streaming bypass, tool collapse state in key. All 3304 lib tests pass, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2x3ft","depends_on_id":"bd-21mwg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T05:48:24.046792864Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":1092,"issue_id":"bd-2x3ft","author":"codex","text":"Dependency update: actively progressing blocker bd-21mwg (interactive modularization). Latest slices validated with focused check/clippy/tests; will continue until bd-21mwg is ready to close so bd-2x3ft can proceed unblocked.","created_at":"2026-02-13T10:25:54Z"},{"id":1097,"issue_id":"bd-2x3ft","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"2026-02-13 codex: blocker update — continuing bd-21mwg modularization (selected via bv robot triage) to unblock this P0 bead.  {"id":"bd-2x7","title":"E2E interactive smoke (tmux) + detailed logs","description":"Goal:
- Add a scripted E2E smoke test for interactive mode using tmux (per AGENTS.md), with detailed logs and captured panes.

Scope:
- Launch pi in tmux with fixed dimensions (80x24).
- Send a prompt, capture output, and verify key UI events.
- Capture logs/pane snapshots as test artifacts.
- Validate clean shutdown and no orphaned processes.

Logging Requirements:
- Use CLI E2E harness (bd-1wc) + TestLogger (bd-3ml).
- Log tmux commands, timings, pane captures, VCR_MODE, and cassette name/path.
- Save pane snapshots + logs on failure for debugging.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Deterministic run using VCR playback and stable terminal dimensions.
- Logs include tmux commands, pane captures, and assertions.
- Tests pass on CI environments that support tmux (skip with clear log if unavailable).","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-2x78","title":"No-mock: provider error-path tests via VCR/real HTTP","description":"# Goal
Eliminate MockHttpServer usage in provider error-path tests by exercising real HTTP code paths with deterministic fixtures (VCR playback or a real local HTTP server).

# Background
`tests/error_handling.rs` and `tests/provider_error_paths.rs` currently use `TestHarness::start_mock_http_server()` to synthesize error responses. This is deterministic but still a mock. The no-mock policy prefers VCR playback of recorded real interactions or an actual local HTTP server that runs the real client stack without fake providers.

# Scope
- Replace each MockHttpServer usage with: (a) VCR playback cassettes recorded from real provider endpoints OR (b) a real local HTTP server that uses the production client stack and real HTTP handling (not a mock dispatcher).
- Preserve coverage for: timeouts, 4xx/5xx, malformed SSE chunks, invalid JSON, and retry handling.
- Emit JSONL logs + artifact index (requests/responses, cassette id, error classification).
- If any mock remains, document it explicitly in `docs/TEST_COVERAGE_MATRIX.md` with a rationale + sunset plan.

# Files
- `tests/error_handling.rs`
- `tests/provider_error_paths.rs`
- `tests/common/harness.rs` (only if a real local HTTP helper is needed)
- `tests/fixtures/vcr/**`

# Acceptance
- No MockHttpServer usage remains in these tests OR any remaining usage is allowlisted with a clear rationale.
- Tests remain deterministic under CI with JSONL logs + artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:26:25.283054429Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T08:38:24.788892103Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T08:38:24.788803007Z","close_reason":"Removed remaining MockHttpServer usage by adding base64 VCR body chunks for invalid UTF-8.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":305,"issue_id":"bd-2x78","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed: Added 16 new VCR-based error-path tests (HTTP 400×4 providers, 403×3, 500×2, SSE error event×1, empty body×2, malformed JSON×2 in provider_error_paths). Total: 50 tests in error_handling.rs + 10 in provider_error_paths.rs. One MockHttpServer test allowlisted (invalid UTF-8 injection). Updated TEST_COVERAGE_MATRIX.md with accurate VCR-only status.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:23:27Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2xc","title":"Unit tests: PiJS stdlib shims + JS boundary","description":"# Goal
Comprehensive **unit tests** for PiJS shims and the Rust↔QuickJS boundary.

# Scope / Deliverables
## 1) PiJS connector shims
- Test `pi.exec`, `pi.http`, `pi.session`, `pi.ui`, `pi.events`, `pi.log` surfaces.
- Verify error mapping (timeouts, denied capability, IO errors).
- Confirm consistent behavior under deterministic seeds/clock overrides.
- Include structured log assertions for key flows.

## 2) Node compatibility shims required by pinned sample
- Unit tests for `pi:node/*` modules (fs/path/os/url/crypto) and globals (process/Buffer) used by the 16/16 sample set.
- Unit tests for `pi:node/child_process` subset behaviors used by sample deps.
- Unit tests for PiWasm `globalThis.WebAssembly` bridge behavior (compile/instantiate, memory limits, trap mapping) where needed by sample.

# Why
- Unit tests isolate regressions faster than full E2E.
- These tests are the fast gate that protects the promise: **16/16 sample extensions run unmodified**.

# Non-goals
- Performance benchmarking (covered elsewhere).

# Notes
- Ordering semantics tests live in bd-39u.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
- [ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
- [ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
- [ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
- [ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
- [ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes

1) pi.* connector shims (JS boundary)
- pi.exec: spawn/timeout/cancel/error mapping
- pi.http: allow/deny policy, size/timeouts, deterministic VCR/stubs
- pi.session: get/set name/state, branch queries (once session connector is wired)
- pi.ui: confirm/select/input/editor request/response mapping (bd-2hz)
- pi.events/log: correlation ids, ordering invariants

2) node:* shims (only what sample corpus uses)
- node:fs (promises-first), node:path, node:os, node:url
- globals: process + Buffer
- crypto subset (hash + randomBytes) as required

3) child_process subset
- spawn/exec/kill mapping onto exec connector (policy-gated)

4) PiWasm bridge
- WebAssembly.instantiate + Memory + trap mapping + limits (blocked by bd-1ry)

Tests should assert structured logs at boundaries (shim->connector calls) to keep evidence binder reproducible.","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:11:48Z"},{"id":742,"issue_id":"bd-2xc","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Done. PiJS shims tested via: npm_module_stubs.rs (38 tests), node_fs_shim tests (25 tests), extensions_property.rs (13 proptest suites), ext_conformance_negative.rs (30 security tests), ext_conformance_guard.rs. 187/223 extensions pass conformance proving shim correctness.","created_at":"2026-02-07T07:02:58Z"}]}
